questions.question: Which of the following are true about cells?

choices.text: Prokaryotes have a nucleus.
They are the basic units of life.
Eukaryotic cells include bacteria.
Mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell.
They don't contain genetic material.

questions.question: What structure exists in all cells?

choices.text: Cell walls
Cell membrane
Nucleus
None of the above

questions.question: Are electrons positively, negatively, or neutrally charged?

choices.text: Neutrally charged
Negatively charged
Positively charged

name: Social and Ethical issues in Computer Science
subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What would a moral realist say about an ethical statement such as “indiscriminate killing is evil”?

choices.text: It is an expression of the speaker’s emotions
It is stating a moral truth that exists in the universe
It is a moral statement that is established by society’s attitudes
It is a recommendation for moral behavior

questions.question: Which of the following statements about rule utilitarianism is false?

choices.text: Both act and rule utilitarianism suffer from many of the same weaknesses
Rule utilitarianism is not susceptible to decisions that favor one’s self-interest
Rule utilitarianism can lead to “rule worship“ where people blindly follow rules
Rule utilitarianism is equivalent to Kantianism

questions.question: The quote: “Act in such a way that you treat humanity, whether in your own person or in the person of any other, never merely as a means to an end, but always as an end in itself” is:

choices.text: Kant’s first formulation of the categorical imperative
Kant’s second formulation of the categorical imperative
The Equality Principle from Rawls’ Theory of Justice
The fortune in Dr. Wong’s fortune cookie from Panda Express

questions.question: Suppose you are in a situation in which two different actions result in two conflicting virtues. What does virtue theory say to do?

choices.text: Virtue theory doesn’t say how to resolve the conflict
Pick the action with the virtue that affects more people
Pick the action with the virtue that is more developed in you
Pick the action with the virtue that is less developed in you

questions.question: Suppose Andy the Act Utilitarian has to decide how to act. He has a utility function that guides his actions towards the same actions that Vera, the Virtue Theorist would choose to do in every situation. Would Vera consider Andy to be acting ethically, assuming she knows that Andy acts according to Act Utilitarianism?

choices.text: Yes
No
